title: Swiss Ribbons Pvt. Ltd. v. Union of India
legal_principles: Distinction between financial and operational creditors is reasonable
summary: Constitutional validity of IBC upheld. The Court held that the differentiation between financial creditors (banks/NBFCs) and operational creditors (suppliers/employees) is constitutionally valid and based on intelligible differentia. IBC's object of revival and resolution over liquidation upheld.
key_holdings: Distinction between financial and operational creditors is constitutionally valid under Article 14
Resolution preferred over liquidation serves legitimate state aim
Section 29A disqualification of promoters is valid
Time-bound CIRP process is constitutionally valid
why_important: Upheld the constitutional validity of IBC — foundational judgment for India's insolvency regime.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Foundation for understanding India's insolvency law, NPA resolution, and creditor hierarchy.
